title: Intensely Briny Mexican Smoked Turkey Neck Cochinita Pibil
description: A briny, crispy, crowd-pleasing Mexican dish featuring smoked turkey neck, inspired by Yucatecan cochinita pibil, made in a kadhai with ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients and smoked to perfection in a tandoor.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large kadhai, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the achiote paste, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the smoked turkey neck to the kadhai, turning to coat evenly with the spice mixture.
Add the lime and orange juice to the kadhai, bringing the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 2 hours, or until the turkey is tender and falling off the bone.
Remove the kadhai from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Shred the turkey neck into bite-sized pieces and serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro and lime wedges.
